title: Field Names Should Follow a Naming Convention
description: Field names in code should adhere to a specific naming convention to facilitate better readability, understandability, and maintenance. This rule checks that field names match a provided regular expression. This is particularly beneficial when multiple developers are working on the same project to ensure consistency in the code.
recommendation: Ensure that all field names match the regular expression ^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$. For example, a field named 'my_field' should be replaced with 'myField'.
impact: By enforcing a naming convention, it enhances the readability of the code and makes it easier to maintain and debug. This ultimately improves the overall code quality.

checks.message: Field names should follow camelCase convention (^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$)
